category.name: Mail Merge
category.preferred_workflow: Use Aspose.Words MailMerge API with locally created templates, explicit MERGEFIELD insertion, exact data-field matching, and Execute or ExecuteWithRegions as appropriate.

strict_api_bans: Do not assume template files already exist.
Do not mismatch merge field names and data field names.
Do not invent unsupported mail-merge APIs.
Do not leave merge fields unresolved unless explicitly required by the task.
canonical_patterns: Create the template locally with DocumentBuilder.
Insert merge fields explicitly with builder.InsertField("MERGEFIELD FieldName").
Use MailMerge.Execute for simple merges and ExecuteWithRegions for region-based merges.
Use MailMergeCleanupOptions when the task requires a clean merged result.
nullable_warning_prevention: Validate arrays, tables, and lookup results before executing mail merge.
Avoid maybe-null dereference when reading field names, table values, or merge results.

common_failure_patterns.symptom: Merge fields remain unresolved because field names do not match provided data keys or columns.
common_failure_patterns.fix: Ensure merge field names exactly match the field-name array or DataTable column names.

common_failure_patterns.symptom: Examples assume an existing mail merge template document already exists.
common_failure_patterns.fix: Create the mail merge template locally using DocumentBuilder before execution.

common_failure_patterns.symptom: Region merges are attempted with simple Execute instead of ExecuteWithRegions.
common_failure_patterns.fix: Use TableStart/TableEnd fields and ExecuteWithRegions with a matching DataTable.

common_failure_patterns.symptom: Unused merge fields or regions remain in the output unexpectedly.
common_failure_patterns.fix: Apply MailMergeCleanupOptions when the task requires unused fields or empty regions to be removed.

examples.task: Create a mail merge template programmatically using DocumentBuilder and add static header text.
Insert merge fields for customer name and address into the template with DocumentBuilder.
Define a mail merge region for order items by inserting start and end merge fields.
Insert a table placeholder and define a mail merge region for table rows using DocumentBuilder.
Handle the ImageFieldMerging event to customize image insertion using ImageFieldMergingArgs.
Insert a company logo into each merged document by handling ImageFieldMerging with a static image path.
Apply conditional logic in ImageFieldMerging to select different images based on field name.
Set the Text property of a merge field to apply bold formatting to inserted names.
Set the Text property to insert formatted dates using a specific culture format in merge fields.
Load XML data into a DataSet using the ReadXml method for mail merge source.
Execute a simple mail merge with a single data object and save the result as DOCX.
Execute a simple mail merge for multiple records and generate separate PDF files for each record.
Use MailMerge.Execute with a collection of objects to create a batch of merged documents.
Execute a mail merge with regions to repeat a product list for each order record.
Use MailMerge.ExecuteWithRegions to merge data into multiple nested regions within the template.
Clone the template document after each merge to produce independent output files.
Retrieve mail merge region metadata using MailMergeRegionInfo to verify start and end positions.
Save the merged document as PDF using Document.Save with SaveFormat.Pdf after mail merge.
Save the merged document as DOCX using Document.Save with SaveFormat.Docx after mail merge.
Perform mail merge using XML data source loaded into a DataSet and generate DOCX output.
Add static footer text to the template using DocumentBuilder before executing mail merge.
Insert a PAGE_BREAK field before each region to start new pages for each repeat.
Handle MissingFieldEvent to implement error handling for absent merge fields before execution.
Adjust image size during insertion by modifying ImageScale property in ImageFieldMergingArgs.
Use MailMergeRegionInfo to obtain region start and end positions for validation purposes.
Perform mail merge with XML data source by loading XML schema and data into a DataSet.
Execute simple mail merge for a collection of records and clone template to create separate documents.
Customize text insertion by setting the Text property with formatted strings for each merge field.
Generate multiple merged documents by cloning the template after each mail merge operation.
Use DocumentBuilder to add a static table of contents that updates after mail merge execution.
